content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves sharply to the left ahead with the car on the right side of the track, still in recovery mode needing to turn left significantly.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 83, target = 83 + 5 = 88
- Waypoint 88: x=7.0997, y=1.1461
- Car position: x=1.0526, y=4.4442
- Bearing = atan2(1.1461 - 4.4442, 7.0997 - 1.0526) = atan2(-3.2981, 6.0471) = atan2(-3.298, 6.047) ≈ -28.6°
- Current heading: -151.91°
- Delta = -28.6 - (-151.91) = +123.31° → normalized = +123.31°

Still very large positive delta — must continue maximum left steering.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Car at x=1.05, y=4.44 heading -151.9° (facing southwest) while target waypoint 88 is at bearing -28.6°, giving delta +123° — must continue maximum left steering at minimum speed to complete the U-turn recovery.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Car in prolonged U-turn recovery near x=1.0-1.3, y=4.4-4.6 for many steps — heading ~-150° to -180° facing west/southwest",
    "Track runs roughly east (positive X direction) near waypoints 80-90",
    "Large positive delta (~120°+) requires maximum left steering (25°) at low speed (0.75)",
    "Car is executing a very slow U-turn recovery — must persist with max left steer",
    "is_left_of_center=false means car is right of center — acceptable during recovery",
    "Track width 0.76m — limited margin, must stay on track during realignment",
    "Progress ~69.5% near waypoints 82-83, track curves left ahead",
    "Visual confirms sharp left curve ahead — car on right side of track near outer edge",
    "Keep max left steering until heading approaches target bearing (~-20° to 0°)",
    "Once heading aligns with track direction (~-20° to 0°), reduce steering and increase speed"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.167, 1.127, 1.086, 1.053, 1.053],
    "y": [4.562, 4.529, 4.485, 4.444, 4.444],
    "yaw": [-166.048, -161.764, -156.236, -151.913, -151.913]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": 123.31,
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": 25.0
}
```
